segments.title: Australia's living naval heritage
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke closes his Australian trip by explaining why the country's extensive naval heritage rewards a visit and still needs active public support.
segments.body: This closing reflection presents Australia's naval history as extensive, living, and worth travelling to encounter. Dr. Clarke stresses that the heritage is supported and loved by people who actively fight for it. He also warns that, like historical resources elsewhere, it remains threatened when decision-makers do not understand its value. The passage therefore links preservation to continued public attention rather than treating survival as guaranteed. Open this moment for a concise assessment of both the strength and vulnerability of Australian naval heritage.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke calls Australia's naval history spectacular, living, supported, and loved, while urging support for those protecting it from neglect.
